review_contract.target_scope: workflow:execute dry-run classification and state preview

findings.title: resume_command renders user-controlled feature slug without shell quoting
findings.hypothesis: A crafted feature slug can inject shell metacharacters into the generated resume command. The CLI does not execute the command itself, but agents/operators may copy or automate the string.
findings.attack_path: Run workflow:execute dry-run with a slug containing a shell separator and inspect the returned resume_command.
preconditions: Attacker can influence or propose the feature slug passed to workflow:execute.
A human operator or automation executes the returned resume_command as a shell command.
evidence: Observed resume_command: aioson workflow:execute C:\dev\aioson --feature=poc;echo AIOSON_POC --tool=claude
Affected code built resumeCommand with raw ${targetDir}, ${slug}, and ${tool} interpolation in src/commands/workflow-execute.js.
Dev fix evidence: PoC now returns resume_command: aioson workflow:execute 'C:\dev\aioson' --feature='poc;echo AIOSON_POC' --tool='claude'.
Regression test added: workflow:execute: resume command quotes user-controlled arguments.
findings.impact: If the generated command is copied into a shell or executed by automation, injected shell syntax after the feature slug can run as a separate command in the local environment.
findings.suggested_fix: Render resume commands with shell-safe quoting for every dynamic argument, or return a structured command array alongside a display string and ensure agents use the structured form for execution.
findings.fix_summary: Added local CLI argument quoting for dynamic command-display arguments and applied it to resume_command plus gate:approve feature arguments.

evidence: PoC resume_command contains --feature='poc;echo AIOSON_POC' and does not contain unquoted --feature=poc;echo AIOSON_POC
